headline: Indigenous land titles take average 12 years to process
context: Orang Asli communities in Peninsular Malaysia wait an average of 12 years for native land title processing through the Department of Orang Asli Development.

cards.big: Orang Asli land title processing takes average 12 years through JAKOA
cards.sub: 230 communities have pending applications dating back to 2010 or earlier

cards.big: Aboriginal Peoples Act 1954 Section 6 empowers state authority over Orang Asli land
cards.sub: Community consent not legally required for land redesignation or gazette changes

cards.big: UNDRIP Article 26 recognises indigenous peoples' right to lands they traditionally own
cards.sub: 12-year processing time effectively denies this right through administrative delay

cards.big: 78 Orang Asli villages lost land to development while titles were still pending
cards.sub: Communities displaced before their legal claims could be resolved

cards.big: A 12-year wait for land titles is dispossession disguised as bureaucracy
JAKOA annual report showed only 34 titles issued in 2024 against 890 pending
